meta.name: PANAS Positive and Negative Affect Schedule
meta.description: The PANAS is a 20-item scale measuring Positive Affect (PA) and Negative Affect (NA).
meta.author: Watson, Clark & Tellegen (1988)
meta.reference: Watson D, Clark LA, Tellegen A. Development and validation of brief measures of positive and negative affect. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1988;54(6):1063-1070.
meta.instruction: This scale consists of words describing feelings and emotions. Indicate to what extent you felt this way during the past week.

commonChoices.label: Very slightly or not at all

ranges.label: Low Overall Affect
ranges.description: Your overall emotional engagement appears reduced, with lower levels of both positive and negative affect. This pattern may reflect emotional numbing, disengagement, or a period of low emotional intensity. Exploring activities that promote emotional vitality may be helpful.
suggestions: Engage in activities that have previously brought you joy or excitement
Practice identifying and labeling your emotions throughout the day
Consider whether this reflects a temporary state or a persistent pattern worth exploring with a professional
Try novel experiences to stimulate emotional engagement

ranges.label: Moderate to High Affect
ranges.description: Your emotional engagement is within a typical range. Review your positive and negative affect subscale scores for a more nuanced picture of your emotional balance. The ideal pattern involves high positive affect combined with low negative affect.
suggestions: Review your subscale scores — aim for high positive affect and low negative affect
Practice activities that boost positive emotions such as exercise, socializing, and pursuing hobbies
If negative affect is elevated, consider stress management techniques like deep breathing or progressive relaxation

positive_affect.label: Low
positive_affect.description: Your positive affect is low, indicating reduced experiences of enthusiasm, alertness, and active engagement. Low positive affect is a hallmark of depression and may signal anhedonia — difficulty experiencing pleasure. Consider activities that promote positive emotions.

positive_affect.label: Moderate
positive_affect.description: Your positive affect is moderate. You experience some positive emotions but there is room for growth. Behavioral activation — scheduling enjoyable and meaningful activities — can help boost positive emotional experiences.

positive_affect.label: High
positive_affect.description: Your positive affect is strong, indicating frequent experiences of enthusiasm, interest, and active engagement with life. High positive affect is associated with greater resilience, better social relationships, and improved physical health.

negative_affect.label: Low
negative_affect.description: Your negative affect is low, indicating minimal distressing emotions such as guilt, fear, or hostility. Low negative affect suggests good emotional regulation and is associated with greater life satisfaction and psychological health.

negative_affect.label: Moderate
negative_affect.description: Your negative affect is in the moderate range. Some negative emotions are a normal part of the human experience. Monitor whether stress or specific situations are driving elevated negative feelings.

negative_affect.label: High
negative_affect.description: Your negative affect is elevated, indicating frequent experiences of distressing emotions such as anxiety, anger, guilt, or fear. Persistently high negative affect is associated with psychological distress and warrants professional attention.
